qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: PNG eXIf metadata is read and duplicated before size guards, causing memory exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is on the PNG `chunk_eXIf()` metadata path in `src/PIL/PngImagePlugin.py`, while the advisory covers PSD images reaching invalid negative tile extents that are checked in `src/decode.c` and `src/encode.c`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's PSD out-of-bounds write root cause.
evidence: The advisory summary states that a specially crafted PSD image can trigger an out-of-bounds write when loaded.
The upstream fix for commit `54ba4db542ad3c7b918812a4e2d69c27735a3199` adds negative `x0`/`y0` tile-extent checks in `src/decode.c` and `src/encode.c` and adds `psd-oob-write*.psd` regression tests.
In `src/PIL/PsdImagePlugin.py`, PSD layer bounding boxes flow into `_maketile()` and then into `ImageFile._Tile(...)`, which is the code path implicated by the advisory fix.

The only qualifying finding instead describes `Image.open()` on PNG files reaching `PngImagePlugin.chunk_eXIf()` and `ImageFile._safe_read()` to duplicate attacker-controlled EXIF metadata before `Image._decompression_bomb_check(im.size)` runs.
